title: NOC 10011 – Human Resources Managers
description: Human Resources Managers classified under NOC 10011 fall within one of the most senior and in-demand occupational groups in Canada’s immigration system. These professionals play a critical leadership role in recruitment strategy, workforce planning, employee relations, and regulatory compliance—making them attractive candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).
duties: Plan, organize, direct, control, and evaluate HR or personnel departments
Forecast workforce needs with departmental managers
Develop labour-relations policies and negotiate collective agreements
Administer employee development, safety, and language programs
Conduct employee meetings on benefits and compensation
Ensure compliance with legislation such as pay-equity rules
employment_requirements: Occupations in TEER 0 usually require a university degree or college diploma and several years of experience in a related field.
job_outlook: Over the next decade, Canada is projected to create 22,400 new positions for Human Resources Managers, while around 24,700 job seekers are expected to enter the field. For internationally trained professionals—particularly applicants from India—this presents encouraging opportunities when paired with a strategic immigration plan.
labour_market_demand: Human Resources Managers are in high demand across private and public sectors and may also be self-employed. Demand under this TEER level has expanded strongly since the pandemic and is expected to remain high in coming years.
Strong hiring prospects across provinces and territories
Excellent immigration prospects for qualified overseas professionals
Annual earnings commonly range from $57,000 to over $315,860, depending on experience, location, and specialization.
Why Human Resources Managers Choose Canada
Professionals immigrating under NOC 10011 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
What Is NOC 10011 – Human Resources Managers?

NOC 10011 refers to professionals who plan, direct, and oversee an organization’s human-resources functions. They manage recruitment, employee relations, training programs, compensation structures, performance evaluation systems, and workplace policies.

These managers ensure compliance with employment laws, develop HR strategies, resolve disputes, support organizational planning, and guide HR teams—playing a key role in shaping company culture and meeting workforce needs.
benefits: In addition to salary, many employers provide:
Dental, medical, and life insurance coverage
Employer contributions to national pension and employment-insurance programs
additional_information: 10 – Specialized middle management occupations in administrative services, financial and business services, and communication (except broadcasting)
100 – Specialized middle management occupations in administrative services, financial and business services, and communication (except broadcasting)
